description_long: This stone chapel features a rectangular nave with a pitched roof and a prominent central bell tower topped with a small dome and cross. The exterior walls are constructed from rough-hewn stone blocks, giving the building a rustic and historic appearance. On one end, a covered portico with arched openings provides a sheltered entrance, while the opposite end is solid with minimal fenestration. The roof is made of metal or slate, and the bell tower rises above the main structure, capped with a conical dome. The chapel sits on a slightly raised plot with visible ground textures around its perimeter. Architectural details are simple, emphasizing solidity and function over ornate decoration.
description: A rustic stone chapel with a central bell tower, arched portico entrance, and pitched roof, exuding a historic and sturdy appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the stone chapel, the central bell tower, and the arched portico on the right.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the solid stone wall and the bell tower rising above the roofline.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the length of the chapel, the bell tower, and the portico on the left.
description_view_3: Frontal view focusing on the arched portico entrance and the central alignment of the bell tower and roof.
attribute_steeple_or_bell_tower: Present; a central bell tower with a polygonal base, capped by a conical dome and topped with a cross, rises above the main roof.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is set within the arched portico, likely made of wood, rectangular in shape, and painted a dark color. The door is simple, with minimal decorative elements, fitting the rustic Romanesque style.
